behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: The failure is most likely at a module handoff: changed-file collection feeding analyzer selection. A unit test of only a filter function can miss wiring bugs (e.g., wrong path normalization or incorrect inclusion from diff provider). Integration is sufficient with in-process modules and fixture PR diffs. Rejected higher level (System): behavior text does not require real GitHub HTTP or real runner OS behavior; it only states changed-vs-unchanged selection logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline step with a fixture changed-file provider plus a mixed repository snapshot (changed + unchanged files), then assert analyzer invocations occur only for changed files and zero findings are emitted from unchanged files.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is formatter + comment payload assembly across modules, not model behavior or OS/network. A pure unit test of grouping may miss integration defects where severity mapping is lost before comment construction. Rejected higher level (System): behavior text requires grouped content in the comment body, not successful real GitHub API posting; this can be validated in-process by inspecting the outbound payload.
behaviors.test_description: Feed mixed-severity findings through the end-to-end in-process comment-building path and assert the generated PR comment payload has distinct error/warning/info sections with correct membership and counts.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is a runtime/timeout requirement tied to GitHub Actions execution limits. Real failure depends on subprocess execution cost, filesystem I/O, and runner performance characteristics; mocks can hide timeout regressions. Rejected higher levels (Agentic/Workflow): no AI/ML decision steps are involved in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the actual lint step in a CI-like environment on a 200-file fixture PR using the real linter subprocess and measure wall-clock completion; assert total runtime stays below 10 minutes with artifacted timing breakdown.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly mentions a process crash. Correctness depends on real subprocess failure semantics (signal/non-zero exit/partial output) and resilient continuation logic over remaining files. In-process exceptions are not equivalent and can miss real crash handling bugs. Rejected higher levels (Agentic/Workflow): no LLM/model decision is part of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis against a file set where one file is known to crash the real linter subprocess; assert the pipeline records that failure, continues analyzing subsequent files, and produces a final PR-comment payload containing surviving findings.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily deterministic deduplication logic on finding tuples (file, line, rule overlap), with no required OS/network/subprocess/model dependency in the behavior text. A focused unit test can catch real failures in keying/merge logic directly. Rejected higher level (Integration): behavior does not require cross-module orchestration evidence; dedup correctness can be fully exercised at the function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Call deduplication logic with synthetic overlapping findings that share file+line but differ by rule source, then assert exactly one output entry per dedup key and stable severity/content selection rules.
